Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|12=draft}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区|12=draft}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year|12=draft}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=draft}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|12=draft}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|12=draft}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|ja|東武鉄道|12=draft}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|ja|柏駅|12=draft}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:柏神社]]

* {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja=大山祇命}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Great Land Master|lt_ja=大己貴命}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏}}, {{ill|Kashiwa City|ja|柏市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gyūto Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gyūto Tennō|lt_ja=天王様}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=柏神社 | 画像=[[File:Kashiwa-jinja ac (2).jpg|280px]]
拝殿
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | 所在地=[[千葉県]][[柏市]][[柏 (柏市)|柏]]三丁目2-2 | 緯度度=35 | 緯度分=51 | 緯度秒=35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯)=N | 経度度=139 | 経度分=58 | 経度秒=25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経)=E | 地図国コード=JP | 創建=[[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年）頃 | 社格=村社 | 祭神=[[月読命]]
[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]
[[大国主|大己貴命]]
[[少彦名命]]
[[出羽神社|伊氐波神]]
[[ウカノミタマ|稲倉魂命]]
[[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]]
[[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] | 本殿=[[権現造]] | 例祭= | 地図=Japan Chiba }} '''柏神社'''（かしわじんじゃ）は、[[千葉県]][[柏市]][[柏 (柏市)|柏]]三丁目にある[[神社]]。[[羽黒神社]]と[[八坂神社]]を合祀した神社である。旧来、[[牛頭天王|天王様]]として親しまれてきた{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=柏市観光協会|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}}。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 祭神 == ;羽黒神社 * [[月読命]] * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]] *[[大国主|大己貴命]] *[[少彦名命]] *[[出羽神社|伊氐波神]] * [[ウカノミタマ|稲倉魂命]] ;八坂神社 * [[スサノオ|素戔嗚尊]] * [[クシナダヒメ|稲田姫命]] == 由緒 == 宮司: 友野俊政{{Cite web|和書|title=柏市｜宗教法人一覧|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=千葉県|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=千葉県}} * 八坂神社は[[1661年]]（[[寛文]]元年）頃　[[京都市]][[東山区]]祇園町の[[八坂神社]]より迎えられ当地に鎮座。 * 羽黒神社は[[1660年]]（[[万治]]3年）頃　[[山形県]][[出羽三山]]の羽黒神社より柏の羽黒台（当地の近隣）に迎えられたといわれている。 *[[1888年]]（[[明治]]21年）　羽黒神社を当地の境内に遷宮。 *[[1907年]]（明治40年）　羽黒神社を八坂社内に合祀。 *[[1974年]]（[[昭和]]49年）　社殿の改築と同時に「柏神社」と名称を改めている。宗教法人名は「羽黒神社」{{Cite web|和書|title=柏市｜宗教法人一覧|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=千葉県|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=千葉県}}。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|柏神社のパノラマ、秋 (2012年)|代替文=|なし|500x500ピクセル]] * 拝殿 *神楽殿 *社務所 *樹齢300年を超える銀杏の巨木 ファイル:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|鳥居 ファイル:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 ファイル:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|絵馬掛け ファイル:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|銀杏の巨木 == 祭事 == * 節分祭 - 2月3日 * 落語寄席公演 == 交通アクセス == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]・[[東武鉄道]][[柏駅]]より徒歩5分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ 柏神社]（公式ホームページ） * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 柏市／柏神社 - まるごとe! ちば] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=千葉県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしわしんしや}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:柏市の歴史]] [[Category:柏市の建築物]] [[Category:千葉県の旧村社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:羽黒神社]]

{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Great Land Master|lt_ja=大己貴命}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|ja|東武鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|ja|柏駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Footnotes == City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|ja|東武鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|ja|柏駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zukuri|qq=}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Kashiwa City|ja|柏市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa City|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gyūto Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gyūto Tennō|lt_ja=天王様|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyūto Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1888|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|ja|東武鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobu Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|ja|柏駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa Station|qq=}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Kashiwa Shrine | native_name = 柏神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | image = [[File:Kashiwa-jinja ac (2).jpg|280px]]
Haiden
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | address = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kashiwa City|ja|柏市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa City|qq=}} {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}} 3‑chōme 2‑2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} (around {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manji|qq=}} 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Great Mountain Deity|lt_ja=大山祇命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Great Land Master|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Gongen-zukuri|ja|権現造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zukuri|qq=}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Kashiwa City|ja|柏市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa City|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gyūto Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gyūto Tennō|lt_ja=天王様|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyūto Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Great Land Master|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Ward|qq=}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1661|qq=}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Sanzan|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1888|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|ja|東武鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tobu Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|ja|柏駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa Station|qq=}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gyūto Tennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gyūto Tennō|lt_ja=天王様|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyūto Tennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Great Land Master|lt_ja=大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} * Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八坂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashiyama Ward|ja|東山区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Ward|qq=}}, around {{ill|1661|ja|1661年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1661|qq=}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Dewa Sanzan|ja|出羽三山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Sanzan|qq=}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|ja|1660年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1660|qq=}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|ja|1888年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1888|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|ja|1974年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1974|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and {{ill|Tobu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:八坂神社]]

{{infobox religious building | name = Kashiwa Shrine | native_name = 柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kashiwa-jinja ac (2).jpg|280px]]
Haiden
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | address = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]] {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}} 3‑chōme 2‑2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = [[:en:1660|1660]] (around [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]]
* [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]]
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:柏市の建築物]] [[Category:柏市の歴史]]

Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * {{infobox religious building | name = Kashiwa Shrine | native_name = 柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kashiwa-jinja ac (2).jpg|280px]]

{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | address = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]] {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}} 3‑chōme 2‑2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = [[:en:1660|1660]] (around [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

* [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]]

* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:柏市の歴史]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}}

{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | address = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]] {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}} 3‑chōme 2‑2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = [[:en:1660|1660]] (around [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
* [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]]
* [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]]
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Yasaka shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:柏市の歴史]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}}

{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:柏市の歴史]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}}

{{infobox religious building | name = Kashiwa Shrine | native_name = 柏神社 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:Kashiwa-jinja ac (2).jpg|280px]]
Haiden
{{Infobox mapframe|zoom=14|frame-width=260}} | address = [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]] {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}} 3‑chōme 2‑2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = [[:en:1660|1660]] (around [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
* [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]]
* [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]]
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|qq=}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}}

It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} *
