* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11533858}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

* [[:en:Ōyamatsumi|Great Mountain Deity]]
* [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]]
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:柏神社]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11533858}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3‑chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, [[:en:Kashiwa|Kashiwa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]], and has long been venerated as [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gyūto Tennō]]. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Great Land Master]] * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|Higashiyama Ward]], around [[:en:1661|1661]] (1st year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the [[:en:Three Mountains of Dewa|Dewa Sanzan]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] to Hakurodai in the local area, around [[:en:1660|1660]] (Manji 3). * In [[:en:1888|1888]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In [[:en:1907|1907]] (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and [[:en:Tobu Railway|Tobu Railway]]’s [[:en:Kashiwa Station|Kashiwa Station]]. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:柏神社]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11533858}} [[Category:Buildings in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|WD=Q7005|lt=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5‑minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3-chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|WD=Q7005|lt=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5-minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|WD=Q7005|lt=1661|1=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway|1=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station|1=Kashiwa Station}}. * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}}

* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|1=Kashiwa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3-chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City|1=Kashiwa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|WD=Q7005|lt=1661|1=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5-minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway|1=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station|1=Kashiwa Station}}. City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|1=Kashiwa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3-chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11533641}}, {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|WD=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City|1=Kashiwa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|WD=Q7005|lt=1661|1=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|WD=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|WD=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|WD=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5-minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|WD=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway|1=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|WD=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station|1=Kashiwa Station}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:柏神社]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11533858}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kashiwa-jinja]][[ja:柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kashiwa-jinja]][[ja:柏神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kashiwa-jinja]][[ja:柏神社]] [[ja:柏神社]] {{infobox religious building | name = Kashiwa Shrine | native_name = 柏神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg | address = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City|1=Kashiwa}} {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11533641}} 3-chōme 2-2 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 51 | 緯度秒 = 35.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 | 経度分 = 58 | 経度秒 = 25.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 創建 = {{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} (around {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|qid=Q1145295|lt=Manji|1=Manji (era)}} 3) | 社格 = Village shrine | deity =* {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}}
* {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Great Mountain Deity|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
* {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}}
* {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
* {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}} | 本殿 = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|en|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|lt=Gongen-zukuri|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | 例祭 = | 地図 = Japan Chiba | location = {{ill|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|1=Kashiwa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.859806|N|139.97375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} }} '''Kashiwa Shrine''' (柏神社, *Kashiwa‐jinja*) is a Shinto shrine located in 3-chōme, in the area of {{ill|Kashiwa|ja|柏 (柏市)|lt=Kashiwa|lt_ja=柏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwa|1=Kashiwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11533641}}, {{ill|Kashiwa|en|Kashiwa|qid=Q319743|lt=Kashiwa City|1=Kashiwa}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It enshrines the deities of both {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, and has long been venerated as {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gyūto Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the old modern system was that of a village shrine. == Worshipped Deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; From Haguro Shrine * {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja|月読命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ}} * {{ill|Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ooyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Great Land Master|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * {{ill|Ihitama-no-Kami|ja|伊氐波神|1=Dewa Shrine|lt=Ihitama-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|ja|稲倉魂命|1=Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano-Mitama-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; From Yasaka Shrine * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|稲田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}} == History == Head priest: Tomono Toshimasa{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=http://kankou.kashiwa-cci.or.jp/spots/kashiwa-jinjya/|website=Kashiwa City Tourism Association|date=2016-11-15|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja}} * The {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} was brought from the Yasaka Shrine of Gion-cho in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|en|Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1203862|lt=Higashiyama Ward|1=Higashiyama-ku, Kyoto}}, around {{ill|1661|en|1661|qid=Q7005|lt=1661|1=1661}} (1st year of {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}}). * The {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} was brought from the Haguro Shrine of the {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|qid=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} to Hakurodai in the local area, around {{ill|1660|en|1660|qid=Q7001|lt=1660|1=1660}} (Manji 3). * In {{ill|1888|en|1888|qid=Q7829|lt=1888|1=1888}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 21) the Haguro Shrine was relocated within the shrine precincts. * In {{ill|1907|en|1907|qid=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) the Haguro Shrine was enshrined together with the Yasaka Shrine. * In {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 49) the shrine buildings were rebuilt and the name was officially changed to "Kashiwa Shrine" (the infobox parameter “name = 柏神社 | native_name = 柏神社” was updated), though the name of the religious corporation remains "Haguro Shrine".{{Cite web|和書|title=柏神社|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/gakuji/shuukyou/houjin/kashiwa.html|website=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-01|language=ja|last=Chiba Prefecture}} == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-dec7-2012.jpg|thumb|Panoramic view of Kashiwa Shrine in autumn (2012)|alt=|500x500px]] * Haiden (worship hall) * Kaguraden (dance hall) * Shamusho (administrative office) * A giant ginkgo tree over 300 years old File:Kashiwa-jinja-fall-torii-dec7-2012.jpg|Torii gate File:Kashiwa-jinja haiden.jpg|Haiden (worship hall) File:Kashiwa-jinja kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (dance hall) File:Kashiwa jinja - Kashiwa - March 10 2019 02 47 45 956000.jpeg|Ema board File:Kashiwa-jinja-ichou.jpg|Giant ginkgo tree == Festivals == * Setsubun Festival – February 3 * Rakugo performance event == Access == It is a 5-minute walk from {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q499071}} and {{ill|Tobu Railway|en|Tobu Railway|qid=Q1321914|lt=Tobu Railway|1=Tobu Railway}}’s {{ill|Kashiwa Station|en|Kashiwa Station|qid=Q865156|lt=Kashiwa Station|1=Kashiwa Station}}. == Footnotes == == External Links == {{commons|Category:Kashiwa-jinja}} * [https://www.kashiwajinja.com/ Kashiwa Shrine] (Official Homepage) * [https://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7557 Kashiwa City / Kashiwa Shrine – Maruchiba! Chiba] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub-Chiba Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|柏神社|version=102255748|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:柏神社]] {{Commons category|Kashiwa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11533858}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kashiwa, Chiba]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kashiwa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]
